Identification.LongTitle: An Act respecting the prohibition of the importation of goods produced by forced labour
Identification.ShortTitle: Ban on Importing Goods Made with Forced Labour Act
Identification.RunningHead: Ban on Importing Goods Made with Forced Labour Act

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: provides that goods produced by forced labour are prohibited from importation;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: authorizes the Minister of Foreign Affairs to establish a list of goods in respect of which there are reasonable grounds to suspect that they are produced by forced labour; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: provides that a person importing goods on the list must, at the request of a customs officer, provide the Canada Border Services Agency with the required information, failing which the goods are deemed to be prohibited from importation.
It also makes consequential amendments to the Customs Tariff.
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Ban on Importing Goods Made with Forced Labour Act.
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: customs officerâhas the meaning assigned by the definition officer in subsection 2(1) of the Customs Act.â(agent des douanes)

forced labourâhas the meaning assigned by the term âforced or compulsory labourâ in Article 2 of the Forced Labour Convention, 1930 (No. 29) of the International Labour Organization.â(travail forcÃ©)
Ministerâmeans the Minister of Foreign Affairs.â(ministre)

personâincludes a trust, a partnership, a fund, an unincorporated association or organization, a country, a political subdivision of a country and a government, and any department or agency, of a country or political subdivision of a country.â(personne)
prescribedâmeans prescribed by regulation.â(Version anglaise seulement)
producedâincludes grown, assembled, manufactured and mined.â(produit)

Section.Text: This Act is binding on His Majesty in right of Canada or a province.
Goods that are produced wholly or in part by forced labour are prohibited from importation.

Subsection.Text: Subject to the provisions of this Act or its regulations, the Customs Act applies, with any necessary modifications, in respect of the administration and enforcement of this Act or its regulations, and for that purpose a contravention of this Act or its regulations is deemed to be a contravention of the Customs Act.
A customs officer designated by the President of the Canada Border Services Agency may determine whether imported goods are produced wholly or in part by forced labour.

For the purpose of making a determination under subsection (2), a customs officer may detain goods for a period of up to 90 days, or for any longer prescribed period, from the first day on which the goods are detained under this subsection. For greater certainty, this subsection does not affect any other lawful authority to detain or otherwise deal with the goods.

For greater certainty, the Governor in Council may make regulations under paragraph 109.1(3)(a) of the Customs Act designating any provisions of this Act or its regulations for the purposes of subsection 109.1(1) of that Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: No appeal, review or re-determination under Customs Act
Subsection.Text: Any power, duty or function exercised or performed under this Act, including under subsection (1), is not subject to any appeal, review, re-determination or further re-determination under the Customs Act but, for greater certainty, is subject to judicial review under section 18.1 of the Federal Courts Act.
The Minister may, by regulation, establish a list of goods in respect of which there are reasonable grounds to suspect that they are produced wholly or in part by forced labour.
The Minister must specify, in relation to goods on the list, either the person who produces them or the country or region in which they are produced, or both.

Section.Text: The following persons may assist the Minister in establishing the list referred to in section 6 and, for that purpose, the Minister and those persons may disclose information to one another:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an officer or employee of the Canada Border Services Agency;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Agriculture and Agri-Food;

Section.Text: A person who imports goods set out in the list established under section 6 must, at the request of a customs officer, provide the prescribed information to the Canada Border Services Agency in accordance with the regulations.
If a person contravenes section 8, the goods for which information was required to be provided under that section are deemed to be prohibited from importation under section 4.

Subsection.Text: A person who imports goods in contravention of section 4 and the owner of the goods at the time they are imported are jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable to His Majesty in right of Canada for any costs incurred by His Majesty in relation to the detention, storage, transportation or disposal of the goods.
Any amount owing under this section is a debt due to His Majesty in right of Canada.
On the recommendation of the Minister, the Governor in Council may make regulations for the purposes of this Act other than sections 5 and 8 to 10.
Regulations made under subsection (1) may prescribe the information that may be or must not be disclosed under section 7.
On the recommendation of the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness, the Governor in Council may make regulations for the purposes of sections 5 and 8 to 10.
Regulations made under subsection (3) may authorize a customs officer to specify the time within which and the manner in which information must be provided under section 8.

Section.Text: The following definitions apply in this section and in sections 13 to 17.

Definition.Text: decisionâmeans a decision made under paragraph 60(4)(a) of the Customs Act in respect of a determination.â(dÃ©cision)

determinationâmeans a determination made under section 58 of the Customs Act to classify goods as goods under tariff item No. 9897.00.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Customs Tariff that were mined, manufactured or produced wholly or in part by forced labour or child labour.â(dÃ©termination)
reference dayâmeans the day on which this Act receives royal assent.â(date de rÃ©fÃ©rence)

Section.Text: A determination that is made before the reference day, and in respect of which no decision is made before that day, is not subject to any appeal, review, re-determination or further re-determination under the Customs Act.

A request that is made under subsection 60(1) of the Customs Act in respect of a determination before the reference day is void if no decision has been made in relation to the request before that day.

A decision that is made before the reference day is not subject to any appeal, review or further re-determination under the Customs Act unless a notice of appeal from the decision is filed under subsection 67(1) of that Act before that day.

Section.MarginalNote: For greater certainty â judicial review

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, a determination to which section 13 applies and a decision to which section 15 applies are subject to judicial review under section 18.1 of the Federal Courts Act.

If such a determination or decision was first communicated to the party directly affected by it within 90 days before the reference day, it is deemed to have been first communicated to that party on that day.

Section.Text: Sections 67 and 68 of the Customs Act and the Description of Goods of tariff item No. 9897.00.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Customs Tariff, as that Description read immediately before the reference day, continue to apply in relation to a decision if a notice of appeal from the decision is filed under subsection 67(1) of the Customs Act before that day.

Heading.TitleText: Consequential Amendments to the Customs Tariff

Section.Text: Subparagraph 132(1)(m)(i.1) of the Customs Tariff is repealed.

The Description of Goods of tariff item No. 9897.00.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by deleting the reference to âGoods mined, manufactured or produced wholly or in part by forced labour or child labour as those terms are defined in section 2 of the Fighting Against Forced Labour and Child Labour in Supply Chains Act,â.
Sections 8 and 9 come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
